subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Help the Word Workshop crew fix signs by snapping prefixes, suffixes, and base words into place.
lesson.objective: I can identify base words and use common prefixes and suffixes to read and understand new words.
lesson.concept_explanation: A base word carries the main meaning. A prefix goes before a base word and a suffix goes after it. Word parts can change how a word is read and what it means.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may grab too much of the word as the base word or attach a prefix or suffix to the wrong side.
lesson.real_world_connection: Word-part clues help readers understand classroom directions, story words, and science words.
lesson.lesson_summary: Find the base word, name the prefix or suffix, then blend the parts and check the meaning.
lesson.mini_lesson: Read the base word first. Notice any prefix before it or suffix after it. Blend the parts, explain what each part means, and check that meaning in context.

worked_examples.text: Watch: In replay, re- means again and play is the base word.
Demo: In helpful, help is the base word and -ful means full of.
Demo: Build un + safe to make unsafe, which means not safe.

hint_ladder: Find and read the base word that carries the main meaning.
Identify the prefix or suffix and state what it adds to the word.
Blend the parts, then use the sentence or definition to check the meaning.

guided_practice.prompt: Which word remains when you take the prefix re- off reread?
hints: Look at reread and find the part added at the beginning.
The prefix re- comes before the base word and means “again.”
Cover re-. Read what remains and check that reread means “read again.”
guided_practice.explanation: Read is the base word in reread. The prefix re- means “again,” so reread means to read again.
guided_practice.feedback: Yes — Read is the base word in reread. The prefix re- means “again,” so reread means to read again.

question_audio.text: Which word remains when you take the prefix re- off reread?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.prompt: The word unsafe means not safe. What is its base word?
hints: Find the small word inside unsafe that carries the main meaning.
Un- is a prefix, so remove it from the beginning.
Read safe by itself; “not safe” confirms that safe is the base word.
guided_practice.explanation: Safe is the base word. Adding un- changes safe to unsafe, which means “not safe.”
guided_practice.feedback: Yes — Safe is the base word. Adding un- changes safe to unsafe, which means “not safe.”

question_audio.text: The word unsafe means not safe. What is its base word?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.prompt: A cook will preheat the oven. Which part of preheat is the base word?
hints: Notice the prefix pre- at the start of preheat.
Take away pre-, which means “before,” and look at the letters left.
Check your answer: The cook will heat the oven before baking, so heat is the base word.
guided_practice.explanation: Heat is the base word in preheat. The prefix pre- means “before,” so preheat means to heat beforehand.
guided_practice.feedback: Yes — Heat is the base word in preheat. The prefix pre- means “before,” so preheat means to heat beforehand.

question_audio.text: A cook will preheat the oven. Which part of preheat is the base word?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.prompt: What is the base word in slowly?
hints: Look for a familiar describing word at the start of slowly.
The suffix -ly is attached to the end of a base word.
Cover -ly and read slow; slowly tells how someone moves.
guided_practice.explanation: Slow is the base word in slowly. The suffix -ly turns slow into a word that tells how something happens.
guided_practice.feedback: Yes — Slow is the base word in slowly. The suffix -ly turns slow into a word that tells how something happens.

question_audio.text: What is the base word in slowly?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.prompt: Which base word is inside thankful?
hints: Find the ending -ful in thankful.
Remove the suffix -ful from the end rather than changing the middle letters.
Read thank and add “full of”; thankful describes someone who feels thanks.
guided_practice.explanation: Thank is the base word in thankful. The suffix -ful adds the meaning “full of,” as in full of thanks.
guided_practice.feedback: Yes — Thank is the base word in thankful. The suffix -ful adds the meaning “full of,” as in full of thanks.

question_audio.text: Which base word is inside thankful?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.prompt: The puppy is playful. What is the base word in playful?
hints: Listen for the familiar action word at the beginning of playful.
The suffix -ful comes after the base word.
Cover -ful and read play; a playful puppy wants to play.
guided_practice.explanation: Play is the base word in playful. Adding -ful makes playful, meaning full of play or ready to play.
guided_practice.feedback: Yes — Play is the base word in playful. Adding -ful makes playful, meaning full of play or ready to play.

question_audio.text: The puppy is playful. What is the base word in playful?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Remove the suffix -less from cloudless. Which base word is left?
hints: Point to -less at the end of cloudless.
A suffix follows the base word; take away all four letters in -less.
Read cloud and check the meaning: a cloudless sky has no cloud in it.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Cloud is the base word in cloudless. The suffix -less means “without,” so cloudless means without clouds.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — Cloud is the base word in cloudless. The suffix -less means “without,” so cloudless means without clouds.

question_audio.text: Remove the suffix -less from cloudless. Which base word is left?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Which base word did the writer use to make painted?
hints: Look at the ending -ed in painted.
Remove only the past-tense ending, not letters from the base word.
Read paint; adding -ed tells that someone painted earlier.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Paint is the base word in painted. The ending -ed shows that the action of painting already happened.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — Paint is the base word in painted. The ending -ed shows that the action of painting already happened.

question_audio.text: Which base word did the writer use to make painted?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: What is the base word in boxes?
hints: Say boxes and listen for the base word in the first spoken beat.
The plural ending here is -es because box ends in x.
Remove -es and read box; more than one box is boxes.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Box is the base word in boxes. The ending -es makes more than one box and adds a spoken syllable.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — Box is the base word in boxes. The ending -es makes more than one box and adds a spoken syllable.

question_audio.text: What is the base word in boxes?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Which base word joins with -er to make farmer?
hints: Find the person-naming ending -er in farmer.
Take -er off the end and keep the complete base word.
A farmer is a person who works on a farm, confirming farm.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Farm is the base word in farmer. The suffix -er names a person who works on a farm.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — Farm is the base word in farmer. The suffix -er names a person who works on a farm.

question_audio.text: Which base word joins with -er to make farmer?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Mia had to refill her cup. What does re- mean in refill?
hints: Split refill into re- and fill.
Think about what Mia does after her cup becomes empty.
Replace re- with “again”: fill again matches refill.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: The prefix re- means “again.” To refill a cup is to fill it again.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — The prefix re- means “again.” To refill a cup is to fill it again.

question_audio.text: Mia had to refill her cup. What does re- mean in refill?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: A shirt that is untied is not tied. What does un- mean?
hints: Compare tied with untied in the sentence.
The prefix un- can give a base word its opposite meaning.
Say “not tied”; it has the same meaning as untied.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: In untied, the prefix un- means “not.” An untied shirt is not tied.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — In untied, the prefix un- means “not.” An untied shirt is not tied.

question_audio.text: A shirt that is untied is not tied. What does un- mean?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: We watched a preview before the movie. What does pre- mean in preview?
hints: Find pre- before the base word view.
Use the sentence clue about when the preview happened.
Check your answer: Substitute “before”: a view before the movie makes sense.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: The prefix pre- means “before.” A preview is something viewed before the full movie.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — The prefix pre- means “before.” A preview is something viewed before the full movie.

question_audio.text: We watched a preview before the movie. What does pre- mean in preview?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Which word means to pack again?
hints: Start with the base word pack.
Choose the prefix that means “again,” not “not” or “before.”
Blend re- and pack, then check that repack means to pack again.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Repack means “pack again” because re- means “again” and pack is the base word.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — Repack means “pack again” because re- means “again” and pack is the base word.

question_audio.text: Which word means to pack again?
question_audio.label: Read Question

hints: Read the base word fair in each choice.
The needed prefix must change fair to its opposite.
Put un-, meaning “not,” before fair and read unfair.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Unfair means “not fair.” The prefix un- adds the meaning “not” to fair.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — Unfair means “not fair.” The prefix un- adds the meaning “not” to fair.

question_audio.text: Which word means not fair?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Which word means to pay before something is received?
hints: The base word pay names the action.
Look for the prefix that means “before,” rather than “again.”
Combine pre- and pay; prepay matches paying ahead of time.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Prepay means “pay before.” The prefix pre- tells that the paying happens ahead of time.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — Prepay means “pay before.” The prefix pre- tells that the paying happens ahead of time.

question_audio.text: Which word means to pay before something is received?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: In the word rewrite, how does re- change the meaning of write?
hints: Separate rewrite into the prefix re- and the base word write.
Recall what re- adds to an action word.
Try “write again” in place of rewrite; the meanings match.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Re- means “again,” so rewrite means to write something again. The spelling of the base word write stays the same.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — Re- means “again,” so rewrite means to write something again. The spelling of the base word write stays the same.
choices: It means a person who writes.

question_audio.text: In the word rewrite, how does re- change the meaning of write?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: The directions say to unplug the lamp. What does un- tell you to do?
hints: Think about the action plug in and its reverse.
In some action words, un- means to undo or reverse the base-word action.
Check the direction: removing the plug makes the lamp unplugged.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: In unplug, un- means to reverse the action. To unplug a lamp is to remove its plug.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Yes — In unplug, un- means to reverse the action. To unplug a lamp is to remove its plug.

question_audio.text: The directions say to unplug the lamp. What does un- tell you to do?
question_audio.label: Read Question

checkpoint.prompt: A preschool class meets before children begin regular school. What does pre- mean?
hints: Break preschool into pre- and school.
Use the time clue about when this class happens.
Check your answer: Replace pre- with “before”: before school explains preschool.
checkpoint.explanation: The prefix pre- means “before.” Preschool comes before regular school.
checkpoint.feedback: Yes — The prefix pre- means “before.” Preschool comes before regular school.

question_audio.text: A preschool class meets before children begin regular school. What does pre- mean?
question_audio.label: Read Question

checkpoint.prompt: Which word means to use something again?
hints: Find the choice built from a prefix and the base word use.
The prefix re- means “again.”
Blend re- with use and check: reuse means use again.
checkpoint.explanation: Reuse means “use again.” The prefix re- adds “again” to the unchanged base word use.
checkpoint.feedback: Yes — Reuse means “use again.” The prefix re- adds “again” to the unchanged base word use.

question_audio.text: Which word means to use something again?
question_audio.label: Read Question

checkpoint.prompt: Two robins landed nearby. What does -s show in robins?
hints: Use the number word two in the sentence.
The ending -s often marks a plural noun.
Check your answer: Remove -s to get one robin; with -s, robins names more than one.
checkpoint.explanation: The suffix -s makes robin plural. Robins means more than one robin.
checkpoint.feedback: Yes — The suffix -s makes robin plural. Robins means more than one robin.

question_audio.text: Two robins landed nearby. What does -s show in robins?
question_audio.label: Read Question

checkpoint.prompt: Yesterday, we planted seeds. What does -ed show in planted?
hints: Notice the time word yesterday.
The ending -ed often marks an action in the past.
Read plant plus -ed; planted matches an action that already happened.
checkpoint.explanation: The ending -ed shows past time. Planted tells that the action already happened yesterday.
checkpoint.feedback: Yes — The ending -ed shows past time. Planted tells that the action already happened yesterday.

question_audio.text: Yesterday, we planted seeds. What does -ed show in planted?
question_audio.label: Read Question

checkpoint.prompt: The frogs are jumping now. What does -ing show in jumping?
hints: Find the helping word are and the time word now.
The ending -ing can name an action in progress when it follows is, am, or are.
Read are jumping; it tells what the frogs are doing right now.
checkpoint.explanation: In this sentence, -ing works with are to show an action happening now: the frogs are jumping.
checkpoint.feedback: Yes — In this sentence, -ing works with are to show an action happening now: the frogs are jumping.

question_audio.text: The frogs are jumping now. What does -ing show in jumping?
question_audio.label: Read Question

checkpoint.prompt: A restful room is full of rest. What does -ful mean in restful?
hints: Split restful into rest and -ful.
Think about what a calm room provides.
Check your answer: Replace -ful with “full of”: full of rest fits restful.
checkpoint.explanation: The suffix -ful means “full of.” A restful room is full of rest and calm.
checkpoint.feedback: Yes — The suffix -ful means “full of.” A restful room is full of rest and calm.

question_audio.text: A restful room is full of rest. What does -ful mean in restful?
question_audio.label: Read Question

misconception_bank.base_word_confusion: Learner treats the whole changed word as the base word.
misconception_bank.prefix_meaning_confusion: Learner does not connect re-, un-, or pre- to meaning.
misconception_bank.suffix_meaning_confusion: Learner does not connect -s, -ed, -ing, -ful, or -less to meaning.
misconception_bank.word_part_boundary_error: Learner splits the word at the wrong word-part boundary.

game_theme.mission_intro: Collect base words, prefixes, and suffixes to build new words in the Word Workshop.

level_banks.label: Level 1: Base Words
level_banks.focus: identify base words inside words with prefixes and suffixes

Think about what a calm room provides.
Check your answer: Replace -ful with “full of”: full of rest fits restful.
questions.explanation: The suffix -ful means “full of.” A restful room is full of rest and calm.
questions.feedback: Yes — The suffix -ful means “full of.” A restful room is full of rest and calm.

question_audio.text: A restful room is full of rest. What does -ful mean in restful?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: A sleeveless shirt is without sleeves. What does -less mean?
hints: Find the base word sleeve at the start of sleeveless.
The suffix -less tells that something is missing.
Say “without sleeves”; that matches the meaning of sleeveless.
questions.explanation: The suffix -less means “without.” Sleeveless describes something without sleeves.
questions.feedback: Yes — The suffix -less means “without.” Sleeveless describes something without sleeves.

question_audio.text: A sleeveless shirt is without sleeves. What does -less mean?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Which word means more than one dish?
hints: Start with the noun dish and decide whether the sentence needs one or more than one.
After sh, add the plural ending -es.
Blend dish and -es as two spoken beats: dish-es means more than one dish.
questions.explanation: Dishes is the plural of dish. Words ending in sh add -es, which makes another spoken syllable.
questions.feedback: Yes — Dishes is the plural of dish. Words ending in sh add -es, which makes another spoken syllable.

question_audio.text: Which word means more than one dish?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Which word tells that the dog’s bark happened in the past?
hints: Compare the endings attached to bark.
The past-tense ending is -ed.
Read bark plus -ed; barked tells about an action that already happened.
questions.explanation: Barked uses the ending -ed to show that the action happened in the past.
questions.feedback: Yes — Barked uses the ending -ed to show that the action happened in the past.

question_audio.text: Which word tells that the dog’s bark happened in the past?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Which word completes the sentence: ‘The baby is __ at us’?
hints: The helping word is signals an action in progress.
Choose the -ing form of the base word smile.
Drop the silent e, add -ing, and read smiling in the sentence.
questions.explanation: Smiling completes is smiling, an action happening now. The silent e in smile is dropped before -ing is added.
questions.feedback: Yes — Smiling completes is smiling, an action happening now. The silent e in smile is dropped before -ing is added.

question_audio.text: Which word completes the sentence: ‘The baby is __ at us’?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: A colorful kite is full of color. Which suffix gives colorful that meaning?
hints: Find the base word color at the beginning.
Look at the letters added after color.
Replace the ending -ful with its meaning “full of” to verify colorful.
questions.explanation: The suffix -ful means “full of.” Color plus -ful makes colorful, meaning full of color.
questions.feedback: Yes — The suffix -ful means “full of.” Color plus -ful makes colorful, meaning full of color.

question_audio.text: A colorful kite is full of color. Which suffix gives colorful that meaning?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: A careless mistake is made without enough care. Which suffix means ‘without’?
hints: Locate the base word care in careless.
Focus on the ending that changes care’s meaning.
Use -less to say “without care”; that matches careless.
questions.explanation: The suffix -less means “without.” Careless means without enough care.
questions.feedback: Yes — The suffix -less means “without.” Careless means without enough care.

question_audio.text: A careless mistake is made without enough care. Which suffix means ‘without’?
question_audio.label: Read Question

level_banks.label: Level 4: Build and Decode New Words
level_banks.focus: build and decode words from base words, prefixes, and suffixes

questions.prompt: Join re- and start. Which word means ‘start again’?
hints: Read the base word start first.
Place re-, meaning “again,” directly before start.
Blend re + start and check every letter: restart means start again.
questions.explanation: Restart is built from re- and start. The parts join without dropping or changing any letters, and re- means “again.”
questions.feedback: Yes — Restart is built from re- and start. The parts join without dropping or changing any letters, and re- means “again.”

question_audio.text: Join re- and start. Which word means ‘start again’?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Build a word meaning “not known” from un- and known.
hints: Keep the complete word known as the base for this task.
Put the prefix un- at the beginning to add the meaning “not.”
Read unknown and check it against “not known.”
questions.explanation: Unknown is un- plus known. The prefix un- means “not,” so unknown means not known.
questions.feedback: Yes — Unknown is un- plus known. The prefix un- means “not,” so unknown means not known.

question_audio.text: Build a word meaning “not known” from un- and known.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Put pre- before test. Which word names a test given beforehand?
hints: Read the base word test.
Choose the beginning that means “before,” then attach it without a space.
Blend pre + test: pretest names a test that comes beforehand.
questions.explanation: Pretest is formed by joining pre- and test. Pre- means “before,” so a pretest is given before later instruction or testing.
questions.feedback: Yes — Pretest is formed by joining pre- and test. Pre- means “before,” so a pretest is given before later instruction or testing.

question_audio.text: Put pre- before test. Which word names a test given beforehand?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Add -er to teach to name a person who teaches.
hints: Begin with the action word teach.
The person-naming suffix in this word is -er.
Join teach and -er, then read teacher—a person who teaches.
questions.explanation: Teacher is teach plus -er. The suffix -er can name a person who does an action.
questions.feedback: Yes — Teacher is teach plus -er. The suffix -er can name a person who does an action.

question_audio.text: Add -er to teach to name a person who teaches.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Which word is made from hope and -less?
hints: Keep the base word hope in view.
Attach the suffix -less after hope; do not drop the final e.
Read hopeless and check its meaning: without hope.
questions.explanation: Hopeless is hope plus -less. The final e stays because -less begins with a consonant, and -less means “without.”
questions.feedback: Yes — Hopeless is hope plus -less. The final e stays because -less begins with a consonant, and -less means “without.”

question_audio.text: Which word is made from hope and -less?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Add -ing to bake. Which spelling is correct?
hints: Start with bake and notice its final silent e.
Before adding -ing, remove the silent e.
Write bak + ing and blend the parts: baking.
questions.explanation: Baking is the correct form. Drop the silent e in bake before adding the vowel suffix -ing.
questions.feedback: Yes — Baking is the correct form. Drop the silent e in bake before adding the vowel suffix -ing.

question_audio.text: Add -ing to bake. Which spelling is correct?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Add -ed to hop. Which spelling shows that the hopping already happened?
hints: Say hop and listen for its short o sound.
For this short-vowel word, double the final p before adding -ed.
Join hopp + ed and read hopped; it tells about the past.
questions.explanation: Hopped is spelled with two p’s. In the short-vowel word hop, the final consonant doubles before -ed.
questions.feedback: Yes — Hopped is spelled with two p’s. In the short-vowel word hop, the final consonant doubles before -ed.

question_audio.text: Add -ed to hop. Which spelling shows that the hopping already happened?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Which word correctly joins joy and -ful?
hints: Keep every letter in the base word joy.
Add the suffix -ful, which is spelled with one l.
Read joyful and check the meaning “full of joy.”
questions.explanation: Joyful is spelled joy plus -ful. The suffix has one l and means “full of.”
questions.feedback: Yes — Joyful is spelled joy plus -ful. The suffix has one l and means “full of.”

question_audio.text: Which word correctly joins joy and -ful?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Break dislike into its two meaningful parts.
hints: Find the familiar base word like at the end.
Look at all the letters before like; they form the prefix dis-.
Blend dis- and like, then check that dislike means not to like.
questions.explanation: Dislike divides into the prefix dis- and the base word like. Here dis- gives the meaning “not” or “the opposite of.”
questions.feedback: Yes — Dislike divides into the prefix dis- and the base word like. Here dis- gives the meaning “not” or “the opposite of.”

question_audio.text: Break dislike into its two meaningful parts.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Which word correctly adds -es to brush?
hints: Read the last two letters of brush: sh.
To make this noun plural, add -es after sh.
Blend brush + es as two spoken beats: brushes.
questions.explanation: Brushes is brush plus -es. A word ending in sh adds -es to name more than one.
questions.feedback: Yes — Brushes is brush plus -es. A word ending in sh adds -es to name more than one.

question_audio.text: Which word correctly adds -es to brush?
question_audio.label: Read Question

level_banks.label: Mixed
level_banks.focus: Interleave all Prefixes, Suffixes, and Base Words targets.

questions.prompt: Noah will recheck his math. Which two parts help you understand recheck?
hints: Use the sentence to decide what action Noah repeats.
Look for the prefix re- at the beginning and a complete base word after it.
Blend re- + check and replace it with “check again” to verify the meaning.
questions.explanation: Recheck is re- plus check. The prefix means “again,” so Noah will check his math again.
questions.feedback: Yes — Recheck is re- plus check. The prefix means “again,” so Noah will check his math again.

question_audio.text: Noah will recheck his math. Which two parts help you understand recheck?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: The trail is uneven. Which meaning matches uneven?
hints: Find the base word even inside uneven.
The prefix un- often changes a describing word to its opposite.
Substitute “not” for un-: not even matches the bumpy trail.
questions.explanation: Uneven means “not even” because un- means “not” and even is the base word.
questions.feedback: Yes — Uneven means “not even” because un- means “not” and even is the base word.

question_audio.text: The trail is uneven. Which meaning matches uneven?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: We saw the game on a prerecorded video. What does pre- tell when it was recorded?
hints: Separate pre- from recorded.
Use the clue that the finished video was ready to watch.
Check your answer: Replace pre- with “before”; recorded beforehand fits the sentence.
questions.explanation: Pre- means “before.” Prerecorded tells that the video was recorded beforehand, before we watched it.
questions.feedback: Yes — Pre- means “before.” Prerecorded tells that the video was recorded beforehand, before we watched it.

question_audio.text: We saw the game on a prerecorded video. What does pre- tell when it was recorded?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Which word means ‘without a home’?
hints: Start with the base word home.
Choose the suffix that means “without.”
Check your answer: Join home and -less without dropping the e; homeless means without a home.
questions.explanation: Homeless means “without a home.” The suffix -less adds the meaning “without” to home.
questions.feedback: Yes — Homeless means “without a home.” The suffix -less adds the meaning “without” to home.

question_audio.text: Which word means ‘without a home’?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: The class cheered loudly. Which base word is inside loudly?
hints: Ask which word describes the sound before -ly was added.
Remove the suffix -ly from the end.
Read loud and check: loudly tells that the cheering was loud.
questions.explanation: Loud is the base word in loudly. The suffix -ly makes a word that tells how the class cheered.
questions.feedback: Yes — Loud is the base word in loudly. The suffix -ly makes a word that tells how the class cheered.

question_audio.text: The class cheered loudly. Which base word is inside loudly?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Which spelling means ‘run again’?
hints: Find the base word run in every possible form.
Choose re-, the prefix meaning “again.”
Join re + run with no space and read rerun.
questions.explanation: Rerun means “run again.” Re- goes before the unchanged base word run.
questions.feedback: Yes — Rerun means “run again.” Re- goes before the unchanged base word run.

question_audio.text: Which spelling means ‘run again’?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Yesterday, Ava carried the books. Which ending in carried shows past time?
hints: Use yesterday to identify when the action happened.
Look at the final two letters of carried that mark past time.
Check your answer: Remove -ed and notice the spelling change back to carry; -ed is the past ending.
questions.explanation: The ending -ed shows that the action happened in the past. In carried, the y in carry changes to i before -ed.
questions.feedback: Yes — The ending -ed shows that the action happened in the past. In carried, the y in carry changes to i before -ed.

question_audio.text: Yesterday, Ava carried the books. Which ending in carried shows past time?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Which word completes the sentence: ‘The puppies are __ in the yard’?
hints: The helping word are calls for an action happening now.
Choose the form made with the suffix -ing.
Join play + ing and read the whole sentence to verify playing.
questions.explanation: Playing completes are playing and shows an action in progress. Add -ing to the base word play without changing the y.
questions.feedback: Yes — Playing completes are playing and shows an action in progress. Add -ing to the base word play without changing the y.

question_audio.text: Which word completes the sentence: ‘The puppies are __ in the yard’?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: What does kindness mean in ‘Her kindness made the new student smile’?
hints: Find the familiar describing word kind at the start.
The suffix -ness can name a quality or state.
Replace kindness with “the quality of being kind” and reread the sentence.
questions.explanation: Kindness means the quality or state of being kind. The suffix -ness turns the describing word kind into a naming word.
questions.feedback: Yes — Kindness means the quality or state of being kind. The suffix -ness turns the describing word kind into a naming word.

question_audio.text: What does kindness mean in ‘Her kindness made the new student smile’?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: A sign says, ‘Please disconnect the hose.’ What does disconnect mean?
questions.correct_answer: separate the hose from what it is joined to
hints: Locate the base word connect, which means join.
The prefix dis- can reverse the base-word action.
Check your answer: Think of the opposite of connect: separate or disconnect.
questions.explanation: Disconnect means to separate something that is connected. The prefix dis- can mean “apart” or “the opposite of.”
questions.feedback: Yes — Disconnect means to separate something that is connected. The prefix dis- can mean “apart” or “the opposite of.”
choices: separate the hose from what it is joined to

question_audio.text: A sign says, ‘Please disconnect the hose.’ What does disconnect mean?
question_audio.label: Read Question
